current.summary: Designed and planned the content discovery redesign — syllago can now discover AI content in any repo regardless of organization. 45 design decisions, 3 panel reviews, 44 beads created, ready for execution.

activeWork.description: Content discovery redesign: split scanner into manifest reader + intelligent content analyzer with 10 provider-specific detectors
activeWork.progress: Full pipeline complete: brainstorm → design (3 panel reviews, 45 decisions) → plan (23 tasks) → quality review (8 blocking fixed) → validation (8 gaps fixed) → 44 beads created → Phase B analysis (7 plan updates). All gates passed. Ready to execute bead 1 of 44.

recentDecisions: Any repo can be a registry — registry.yaml manifest is the single source of truth, generated by content analyzer when absent
Scanner simplified to manifest-only reader; all intelligence in new analyzer package with 10 provider detectors
Executable content (hooks, MCP) always requires explicit user confirmation regardless of confidence — security invariant
Items keyed by (registry, type, name) not path — survives directory renames without detection heuristics
Command renamed from 'syllago init' to 'syllago manifest generate' to avoid collision with existing init command
Prompts content type collapsed into Rules (adding separate Prompts type has too much blast radius for identical behavior)
CC detector imports from converter/toolmap.go directly — no import cycle, no duplication of event maps
nextSteps: Execute bead syllago-64yfj: [test] Task 1.1 — Extend ManifestItem with optional fields (first bead)
Execute remaining 43 beads in dependency order through the full implementation
Commit research + design + plan artifacts before starting code changes

context.notes: This session was pure design and planning — no code was written. All artifacts are uncommitted docs/plans/research files. The implementation plan has been through quality review (8 blocking issues fixed), validation (8 parity gaps fixed), and Phase B analysis (7 plan updates for hidden blockers). The bead chain has a midpoint checkpoint after Task 8.1 (dedup) and a final validation bead. Task 13.1 (scanner update) can run in parallel with phases 3-12 since it only depends on Task 1.1. Phase 16 was moved before Phase 12 per Phase B finding (12.1 calls AllItems() defined in 16.1).
